title: Sphaeroma glabratum
intro:
There is no plant known as Sphaeroma glabratum. However, I found that Sphaeroma is actually a genus of isopod crustaceans, not plants. 

If you're looking for a plant with a similar name, you might be referring to Sphaeroma glabratum's plant counterpart Sphaeranthus. But Sphaeranthus is a genus of flowering plants, commonly known as globe thistle or globe flower.

Here's a paragraph on Sphaeranthus:

Sphaeranthus is a genus of flowering plants in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as globe thistle or globe flower. The health benefits of Sphaeranthus include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Sphaeranthus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Sphaeranthus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Sphaeroma glabratum is classified within the phylum Arthropoda, specifically within the subphylum Crustacea. It belongs to the order Isopoda and the family Sphaeromatidae. This species is further categorized into the genus Sphaeroma, which is a group of isopods commonly found in marine environments. The classification of Sphaeroma glabratum reflects its unique characteristics and adaptations to its aquatic habitat. Its classification helps scientists understand its evolutionary relationships with other crustaceans and its place within the larger taxonomic framework.
